frameworks.name: online hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: structured motion relations inferred online from volatile visual input

frameworks.name: Expectation-Maximization (online EM)
frameworks.one_line: derived as the algorithmic implementation of hierarchical motion inference

frameworks.name: neural network implementation
frameworks.one_line: model mapped onto motion-sensitive cortical area properties

core_question: How does the visual system online infer the hierarchical structure of motion relations from a volatile stream of input?
key_assumptions: motion structure can be represented hierarchically
the brain performs Bayesian inference over latent motion relations
inference must operate online on a streaming input
key_findings: online EM model reproduces human percepts qualitatively and quantitatively across stimulus classes
single framework spans classical, ambiguous, and illusory motion regimes
model affords a neural network implementation sharing properties with motion-sensitive cortical areas
interpretations: online hierarchical Bayesian inference is a normative origin for human motion structure perception
the framework yields testable predictions for future psychophysics
